response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but he falls critically short on every mandatory requirement for this role. His C++ experience amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project — far below the required 5–10 years. There is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ development, no SQL Server or Oracle exposure (MongoDB is his primary database), and no capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge. His one finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) was built entirely on Node/TypeScript/GraphQL and addressed retail investment onboarding, not trading systems. Even the desirable skills list is a near-miss: Python is absent, STL is unmentioned, and there is no Kafka/MQ or Summit experience.
strengths: Solid CI/CD and DevOps background established at Altom (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory, multi-OS CI servers)
Java development experience mentioned at Altom, satisfying one optional desirable skill
Strong communication skills evidenced by public conference talks, full-day workshops, and open-source community contributions
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project) versus the mandatory 5–10 years
Zero fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking systems exposure throughout the entire career
Database experience is dominated by MongoDB and MySQL; no SQL Server or Oracle work is documented anywhere in the resume
